course_title: Cost Effectiveness in Health and Medicine

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate knowledge about role of economic thinking in public health policy, planning, and priority-setting
Describe and distinguish key economic methods used in public health evaluation
Apply core concepts of economic evaluation to conduct and interpret a basic cost-effectiveness analysis using decision-analytic modelling.
Critically assess the quality, transparency, and policy relevance of published economic evaluations using established reporting standards
Communicate economic evidence clearly and persuasively to diverse stakeholders

assessments.task_description:
Part A: HEAP Report (20%): Develop a Health Economic Evaluation Plan (HEAP) for a selected public health intervention. The HEAP outlines your proposed evaluation design, including the type of economic evaluation, costs and outcomes to be measured, data sources, and analytical approach.

Part B: Viva voce (20%): An individual oral discussion with the teaching team to explore your HEAP in depth. Students must score at least 10 out of 20 (50%) in Part B to pass the course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment consists of six (6) weekly tasks completed from Week 2 to Week 7, each designed to build and test your understanding of the topics covered to that point. Each task is worth 5% of the total course mark. Your final 20% for Assessment 1 is calculated from your four (4) highest-scoring tasks out of six.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.weighting: <dd>40% Part 1: 20% individual report; Part 2: 20% viva voce  </dd>
assessments.task_description:
Part A: HEAP Report (20%): Develop a Health Economic Evaluation Plan (HEAP) for a selected public health intervention. The HEAP outlines your proposed evaluation design, including the type of economic evaluation, costs and outcomes to be measured, data sources, and analytical approach.

Part B: Viva voce (20%): An individual oral discussion with the teaching team to explore your HEAP in depth. Students must score at least 10 out of 20 (50%) in Part B to pass the course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Part A (Health Economic Evaluation Plan) will be submitted via Turnitin.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Report on the economic evaluation of a public health programme
assessments.task_description: A written report presenting the results, findings, and policy recommendations from the economic evaluation of a public health intervention selected in Assessment 2. This assessment builds directly on your HEAP and is the culminating piece of work for the course
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshops

Interactive lectures and tutorials in a workshop format. These will include: student-led discussions, presentations, in-class group work, and practical exercises in Excel. The practical exercise in Excel will start in week 6, and run for 1 hour per week. It is designed to support students to develop their term project.
